items.title: US inflation fell more than expected to 3.5% in June as oil prices tumbled
items.description: Traders rein in bets on Fed rate rises as easing energy costs help tame price surges spurred by Middle East war

items.title: Washington and Tehran send warnings by missile
items.description: The foes are in their most dangerous period of fighting since a fragile April truce. Many of their strikes are designed to send signals

items.title: IBM shares plunge 23% on profit warning
items.description: Company blames sluggish customer spending on AI infrastructure as tech group signals it will miss market expectations

items.title: Democrats are not in great shape
items.description: A fractious opposition is doing everything it can to spurn an open goal

items.title: Wall Street banks post blockbuster profits as equities trading booms
items.description: JPMorgan, Goldman, Citi and BofA kick off earnings season with strong results

items.title: SoftBank’s Son ridicules AI critics for ‘spitting upwards’
items.description: Business leaders unwilling to embrace technology ‘should play the role of spouse’, says controversial investor

items.title: Spain begins Gibraltar airport checks as Brexit reaches the Rock
items.description: Troubled EU entry system will be turned on from Wednesday, with UK territory in effect becoming part of Schengen zone

items.title: RIP the US state department
items.description: America’s diplomats used to have global sway. Now they are besieged by their own president
